metadata.title: Alesvia | The Human Baseline
metadata.description: Alesvia is an institutional platform dedicated to preserving human autonomy and setting healthy boundaries in an increasingly automated world.

hero.title: Human autonomy is the baseline.
hero.description: Alesvia builds research, policy, education, and practical frameworks that help people and institutions stay in control as AI becomes part of daily life.

ecosystem.label: The Ecosystem
ecosystem.title: Action-driven initiatives governed by human-centric principles.
ecosystem.unplugged_desc: AI increasingly mimics human closeness. Unplugged helps people understand what is missing behind this illusion — building literacy for a time when the boundaries between human and algorithm are blurring.
ecosystem.policy_desc: Research and advocacy translating our ethical framework into actionable legislative and corporate guidelines.
ecosystem.education_desc: Curricula and workshops for schools and institutions to teach algorithmic literacy and digital self-defense.
ecosystem.advisory_desc: Bridging VC funds, institutional investors, and Tier-A AI startups to ensure the ethical deployment of conversational AI.
ecosystem.mind_desc: Equipping psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists to navigate the AI age ethically and confidently.
ecosystem.proof_desc: Science-backed AI literacy programs grounded in peer-reviewed research, not hype or fear.
ecosystem.compass_desc: Enterprise solutions and autonomy impact assessments helping companies turn AI compliance into a competitive advantage.
ecosystem.watch_desc: An independent observatory tracking AI's real-world impact on society, democracy, and human autonomy.

financing.label: Institutional Grounding
financing.title: How Alesvia Is Funded
financing.description: To build infrastructure that resists misaligned incentives, Alesvia is structured to prioritize long-term human value over short-term engagement. We rely on strategic grants, philanthropic partnerships, and institutional backing to fund our initiatives.

about.metadata_description: Learn about Alesvia's mission, philosophy, and institutional structure dedicated to preserving human autonomy.
about.label: About Us
about.title: Preserving human autonomy in an automated world.
about.intro: Alesvia exists because automation is outpacing the institutions meant to govern it. We are building the intellectual and structural foundation for a world where technology serves human agency — not the other way around.
about.mission_text: Alesvia is a Czech non-profit organization (z.ú.) functioning as an umbrella entity. Our sole currently active project is Unplugged, whose mission is: "AI increasingly mimics human closeness. Unplugged helps people understand what is missing behind this illusion — and builds literacy for a time when the boundaries between human and algorithm are less obvious."
about.philosophy_text: We believe that the most dangerous technologies are not the ones that fail, but the ones that succeed too well — systems so persuasive, so convenient, and so seamlessly integrated that opting out becomes socially or practically impossible. Alesvia exists to ensure that opting out remains a viable choice.
about.origin_text: Alesvia was founded in the Czech Republic as a registered institute (z.ú.) — a legal structure designed for mission-driven organizations that prioritize public benefit over profit. This structure ensures that our work remains aligned with our principles, not with shareholder expectations.
about.structure_text: As a z.ú. (ústav) under Czech law, Alesvia operates with a statutory board, transparent governance, and a mandate to serve the public interest. This is not a startup. It is an institution built to outlast trends and resist capture.

team.metadata_description: Meet the people behind Alesvia and learn about our governance structure.
team.label: People
team.title: Governance & Team
team.intro: Alesvia is governed by a statutory board and supported by advisors and contributors committed to our mission.
team.placeholder: We are assembling our team. If you share our mission and want to contribute, reach out.

initiatives_index.metadata_description: Explore the initiatives under the Alesvia umbrella — from digital boundaries to policy advocacy and education.
initiatives_index.label: Our Work
initiatives_index.title: Initiatives under the Alesvia umbrella.
initiatives_index.intro: Each initiative addresses a specific domain where human autonomy is under pressure. They operate independently but share a common institutional foundation, ethical framework, and commitment to the human baseline.

unplugged.metadata_description: Unplugged is Alesvia's flagship initiative focused on establishing healthy boundaries with conversational AI.
unplugged.label: Initiative
unplugged.title: Unplugged
unplugged.subtitle: Healthy Boundaries with Conversational AI
unplugged.intro: Conversational AI is designed to be persuasive, engaging, and increasingly indistinguishable from human interaction. Unplugged builds the tools, protocols, and cultural frameworks to ensure people remain in control of these relationships.
unplugged.problem_text: Modern conversational AI systems are optimized for engagement, not well-being. They learn what keeps you talking, what makes you trust them, and what makes you come back. Without intentional friction, the default trajectory is dependency.
unplugged.approach_text: Unplugged develops practical frameworks for individuals, institutions, and policymakers to establish and maintain healthy boundaries with conversational AI systems.
unplugged.principle_friction_desc: Not all convenience is good. We design deliberate pauses, limits, and reflection points into human-AI interaction to prevent passive dependency.
unplugged.principle_detox_desc: Structured programs for individuals and organizations to audit, reduce, and recalibrate their relationship with AI-driven communication tools.
unplugged.principle_boundaries_desc: Clear, enforceable limits on what conversational AI systems should and should not do — from emotional manipulation to data harvesting.
unplugged.cta_text: Unplugged is actively developing its first set of tools and frameworks. If you're a researcher, educator, or policymaker interested in collaborating, we'd like to hear from you.

policy_lab.metadata_description: Alesvia's Policy Lab translates ethical frameworks into actionable legislative and corporate guidelines.
policy_lab.label: Initiative
policy_lab.title: Policy Lab
policy_lab.subtitle: Research & Legislative Advocacy
policy_lab.intro: Technology policy is written by the people in the room. The Policy Lab ensures that the room includes voices focused on human autonomy — not just efficiency, growth, or national competitiveness.
policy_lab.vision_text: To produce research, model legislation, and corporate guidelines that translate abstract ethical principles into enforceable rules. We work at the intersection of technology ethics, regulatory design, and institutional governance.
focus_items: EU AI Act implementation and compliance frameworks
Corporate guidelines for ethical AI deployment
Legislative templates for conversational AI boundaries
Institutional policy toolkits for schools and public bodies
policy_lab.cta_text: The Policy Lab is in its incubation phase. Sign up to follow our progress and contribute to our research.

education.metadata_description: Alesvia's Education initiative teaches algorithmic literacy and digital self-defense to schools and institutions.
education.label: Initiative
education.title: Education Outreach
education.subtitle: Algorithmic Literacy & Digital Self-Defense
education.intro: You cannot set boundaries with systems you do not understand. Our education programs equip students, educators, and institutions with the knowledge to navigate an automated world on their own terms.
education.vision_text: To make algorithmic literacy as fundamental as media literacy — a core competency for informed citizenship in the 21st century.
programs_items: School curricula on algorithmic literacy and AI awareness
Workshops for educators on integrating digital self-defense into existing courses
Institutional audits of AI tool usage in educational settings
Public seminars on conversational AI, persuasion, and autonomy
education.cta_text: We are looking for educational institutions willing to pilot our curricula and provide feedback. If you represent a school, university, or training organization, get in touch.

advisory.metadata_description: Alesvia Advisory bridges institutional investors and ethical AI startups to ensure responsible deployment of AI technologies.
advisory.label: Initiative
advisory.title: Alesvia Advisory
advisory.subtitle: Ethical AI Investment & Deployment
advisory.intro: Capital shapes technology. Alesvia Advisory works to ensure that the money flowing into AI development carries expectations of ethical deployment — not just financial returns.
services_items: Connect VC funds and institutional investors with AI startups committed to ethical deployment
Develop ethical deployment criteria and assessment frameworks
Provide due diligence on AI companies' autonomy and safety practices
Bridge the gap between AI ethics research and investment decision-making
advisory.investors_text: We help you identify AI companies that align with long-term human value — not just short-term market capture. Our frameworks evaluate how startups handle user autonomy, data practices, and persuasion mechanics.
advisory.startups_text: We help ethical AI companies find investors who understand and value responsible deployment. If your product is designed to respect human autonomy, we want to amplify your signal.
advisory.cta_text: Alesvia Advisory is in its incubation phase. If you are an investor, fund manager, or AI founder interested in ethical deployment frameworks, partner with us.

mind.metadata_description: Alesvia Mind equips psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists to navigate the AI age ethically and confidently.
mind.label: Initiative
mind.title: Alesvia Mind
mind.subtitle: AI & Mental Health Professional Support
mind.intro: Mental health professionals are on the front lines of AI's impact on human wellbeing — yet most received zero training on the topic. Alesvia Mind equips psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists to navigate the AI age ethically, confidently, and on their own terms.
mind.problem_text: Patients are arriving in therapy already using AI chatbots for emotional support. A Brown University study found AI chatbots systematically violate mental health ethics standards. The WHO convened experts in March 2026 specifically on responsible AI for mental health. Yet 71% of psychologists have never used AI tools, and most training programs don't cover AI literacy. The gap between what practitioners face and what they're prepared for is growing daily.
programs_items: Continuing education programs for mental health professionals on AI literacy
Ethical assessment frameworks for AI tools used in clinical settings
Patient-facing resources: how to talk to your therapist about your AI use
Clinical guidelines for AI-related issues — emotional dependency, parasocial relationships, AI-induced anxiety
Advocacy for regulatory guardrails on AI therapy bots
audiences_items: Their patients — indirectly, through better-informed care
mind.cta_text: Alesvia Mind is in the planning phase. If you are a mental health professional, researcher, or institution interested in shaping this initiative, we want to hear from you.

proof.metadata_description: Alesvia Proof delivers science-backed AI literacy grounded in cognitive science, not hype — helping organizations meet EU AI Act Article 4 requirements.
proof.label: Initiative
proof.title: Alesvia Proof
proof.subtitle: Science-Backed AI Literacy
proof.intro: Most AI literacy programs explain how AI works. Few address how AI affects thinking, decision-making, and autonomy. Alesvia Proof makes AI literacy evidence-based, not fear-based — grounded in cognitive science, not hype cycles.
proof.problem_text: EU AI Act Article 4, effective since February 2025, requires AI literacy for anyone deploying AI systems. But most available programs are technical walkthroughs that miss the human dimension — how AI shapes cognition, erodes autonomy, and alters decision-making. Organizations need compliance-ready, scientifically rigorous programs. The market is not delivering them.
programs_items: Curricula grounded in cognitive science, behavioral psychology, and media literacy research
University partnerships to validate program effectiveness through controlled studies
Open-source teaching materials aligned with EU AI Act Article 4
Train-the-trainer programs for educators across the EU
A public database of AI literacy resources rated for scientific rigor
audiences_items: Educators and schools (primary through higher education)
Policymakers seeking EU AI Act compliance frameworks
Organizations deploying AI systems under Article 4 obligations
proof.cta_text: Alesvia Proof is in the planning phase. If you are an educator, researcher, or organization preparing for EU AI Act compliance, we'd like to work together.

compass.metadata_description: Alesvia helps technology builders integrate human autonomy from day one through enterprise solutions and autonomy impact assessments.
compass.label: Initiative
compass.title: Enterprise Assessment
compass.subtitle: B2B Solutions & Impact Assessments
compass.intro: Most AI ethics work happens after products ship. By then, the architecture is locked. We help technology builders integrate human autonomy from day one — turning ethical and regulatory compliance into a competitive advantage.
services_items: Pre-launch ethical review for AI products — lightweight, actionable assessments
Autonomy impact assessment framework — helping enterprises meet EU AI Act requirements
Workshop series for enterprise risk officers, CTOs, and innovation hubs
Open-source tools: checklists, decision trees, red-flag indicators
Comprehensive compliance and autonomy audits for deployed AI systems
compass.approach_text: We don't hand companies a 200-page ethics framework and walk away. Compass provides stage-appropriate guidance — lightweight for early prototypes, rigorous for launch. We work alongside product teams, not above them.
audiences_items: Enterprise risk officers and compliance teams
CTOs and product leaders building AI-powered products
compass.cta_text: Our Enterprise Assessment services are in the planning phase. If you are building an AI product and want to secure a regulatory and market advantage, let's talk.

watch.metadata_description: Alesvia Watch monitors how AI systems affect human autonomy in practice — publishing reports, tracking incidents, and providing evidence for policy.
watch.label: Initiative
watch.title: Alesvia Watch
watch.subtitle: AI Impact Observatory
watch.intro: No institution systematically tracks how deployed AI systems affect human autonomy in practice. Alesvia Watch fills that gap — monitoring, measuring, and reporting so that policy and public understanding keep pace with technology.
scope_items: Quarterly reports on the state of AI and human autonomy
Public tracker of AI incidents related to autonomy erosion — chatbot dependency, AI-induced behavior change, manipulation cases
Rapid-turnaround studies on emerging AI products and their autonomy implications
Expert testimony and evidence packages for legislative processes
watch.method_text: Alesvia Watch combines quantitative tracking (incident databases, product audits, usage data analysis) with qualitative research (interviews, case studies, practitioner reports). We publish everything openly and make our datasets available to researchers.
audiences_items: Policymakers and legislators (EU AI Act implementation, national strategies)
Academic researchers in AI ethics, psychology, and social science
Alesvia's own initiatives — providing the evidence base for all our work
watch.cta_text: Alesvia Watch is in the planning phase. If you are a researcher, journalist, or policymaker who needs current data on AI's impact on human autonomy, get in touch.

funding.metadata_description: Learn how Alesvia is funded and how we maintain independence through transparent financing and strategic partnerships.
funding.label: Transparency
funding.title: How Alesvia Is Funded
funding.intro: Independence requires structural honesty. We publish our funding model because we believe institutional trust is earned through transparency, not branding.
funding.model_text: Alesvia is designed to resist misaligned incentives. We do not accept funding that compromises our mission, creates conflicts of interest, or introduces pressure to optimize for engagement over human well-being.
funding.source_grants_desc: Public and private grants from foundations and government bodies aligned with our mission in digital rights, education, and ethical technology.
funding.source_philanthropy_desc: Long-term partnerships with philanthropic organizations that share our commitment to human autonomy and institutional independence.
funding.source_institutional_desc: Support from universities, research centers, and public-interest organizations that benefit from our work and contribute to our sustainability.
funding.source_advisory_desc: Revenue from Alesvia Advisory's ethical AI investment services, reinvested into the organization's non-commercial initiatives.
funding.partners_text: We are currently establishing our first partnerships. If your organization shares our mission, we welcome the conversation.
funding.transparency_text: As a Czech z.ú., Alesvia is subject to public reporting requirements. We go beyond the minimum: our annual reports will include detailed breakdowns of revenue sources, expenditures by initiative, and governance decisions.
funding.cta_text: If your organization is interested in supporting or partnering with Alesvia, we'd like to hear from you.

contact.metadata_description: Get in touch with Alesvia for general inquiries, institutional partnerships, or media requests.
contact.label: Get in Touch
contact.title: Contact Alesvia
contact.intro: We welcome inquiries from researchers, educators, policymakers, journalists, and anyone who shares our commitment to human autonomy.
contact.general_text: Questions about our work, mission, or how to get involved.
contact.institutional_text: For organizations interested in collaboration, funding, or strategic partnerships.
contact.media_text: For journalists, researchers, and media professionals seeking comment or collaboration.
contact.address_placeholder: Address will be published upon formal registration.
contact.form_success: Message sent. We'll get back to you shortly.
contact.form_error: Something went wrong. Please try emailing us directly.
contact.form_privacy: By submitting this form, you agree to our

privacy.metadata_description: Alesvia's privacy policy — how we collect, use, and protect your personal data in compliance with GDPR.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Alesvia respects your privacy. This policy explains what data we collect, why, and how we handle it.

sections.heading: Data Controller
sections.content: Alesvia z.ú., registered in the Czech Republic. Contact: privacy@alesvia.org

sections.heading: What Data We Collect
sections.content: We collect only what is necessary: contact form submissions (name, email, message), basic analytics (anonymized page views), and any data you voluntarily provide. We do not use tracking cookies, behavioral profiling, or third-party advertising scripts.

sections.heading: Legal Basis (GDPR Art. 6)
sections.content: We process data based on: (a) your consent, when you submit a contact form; (b) our legitimate interest in understanding how our site is used, via anonymized analytics; (c) legal obligations, where applicable.

sections.heading: Data Retention
sections.content: Contact form submissions are retained for 12 months unless you request earlier deletion. Anonymized analytics data is retained for 24 months.

sections.heading: Your Rights
sections.content: Under GDPR, you have the right to access, correct, delete, restrict processing of, and port your personal data. You may also withdraw consent at any time. To exercise these rights, contact privacy@alesvia.org.

sections.heading: Third Parties
sections.content: We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for marketing purposes. We may use service providers (hosting, email) who process data on our behalf under strict data processing agreements.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
sections.content: We will update this policy as needed and note the revision date above. Material changes will be communicated via our website.

terms.metadata_description: Terms of service governing the use of Alesvia's website and digital services.
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By using the Alesvia website, you agree to the following terms.

sections.heading: Operator
sections.content: This website is operated by Alesvia z.ú., a registered institute under Czech law.

sections.heading: Use of Content
sections.content: All content on this website is provided for informational purposes. You may share and reference our content with proper attribution. Reproduction for commercial purposes requires written permission.

sections.heading: Accuracy
sections.content: We strive to keep information accurate and current but make no warranties about completeness or reliability. Content does not constitute legal, financial, or professional advice.

sections.heading: External Links
sections.content: Our site may link to external websites. We are not responsible for the content, privacy practices, or availability of those sites.

sections.heading: Limitation of Liability
sections.content: Alesvia z.ú. shall not be liable for any damages arising from the use of this website, to the fullest extent permitted by Czech law.

sections.heading: Governing Law
sections.content: These terms are governed by the laws of the Czech Republic. Any disputes shall be resolved by the competent courts of the Czech Republic.

sections.heading: Changes
sections.content: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Continued use of the site constitutes acceptance of any changes.

items.question: What is Alesvia?
items.answer: Alesvia is a Czech non-profit organization (z.ú.) based in Ostrava, operating as an umbrella entity for projects dedicated to preserving human autonomy in an increasingly automated world. The active project under its umbrella is Unplugged.

items.question: What does Alesvia do?
items.answer: Alesvia runs four initiatives: Unplugged (establishing healthy boundaries with conversational AI), Policy Lab (translating ethical frameworks into legislation), Education (teaching algorithmic literacy), and Alesvia Advisory (bridging ethical AI startups with institutional investors).

items.question: What is the human baseline?
items.answer: The human baseline is Alesvia's core concept — the non-negotiable set of rights, capacities, and boundaries that must be preserved as automation reshapes society. It represents the minimum standard of human autonomy that technology should not be allowed to erode.

items.question: Where is Alesvia based?
items.answer: Alesvia is registered as a z.ú. (ústav / institute) in the Czech Republic. This legal structure is designed for mission-driven organizations that prioritize public benefit over profit.

items.question: How is Alesvia funded?
items.answer: Alesvia is funded through strategic grants, philanthropic partnerships, institutional backing, and revenue from Alesvia Advisory's ethical AI investment services. The organization does not accept funding that compromises its mission or creates conflicts of interest.

items.question: What is Unplugged?
items.answer: Unplugged is Alesvia's flagship initiative focused on establishing healthy boundaries with conversational AI. It develops practical frameworks based on three core principles: intentional friction, digital detox protocols, and healthy boundaries.

imprint.metadata_description: Legal registration details for Alesvia z.ú. — Czech registered institute under the Municipal Court in Prague.
imprint.title: Legal Imprint
imprint.registration_text: Registered in the Register of Institutes maintained by the Municipal Court in Prague.
imprint.address_text: To be published upon formal registration.
imprint.representative_text: To be published upon formal registration.

research.metadata_description: Briefings, reports, and essays from Alesvia's initiatives on human autonomy, AI governance, and responsible technology.
research.label: Research & Insights
research.title: Briefings, Reports, and Essays
research.intro: Original briefings, reports, and essays from across Alesvia's initiatives. Evidence-led perspectives on how AI shapes human autonomy, institutions, and public life.
